scenario: A custom Outbound Connector built with the SDK has a property in its element template marked with binding: { type: "zeebe:input", name: "apiUrl" }. The team is unsure what this binding type does. What does zeebe:input binding type mean?
question: What does zeebe:input binding type mean?

options.text: @Variable annotation on a Spring Zeebe Job Worker method parameter creates a Zeebe Input Variable mapping on the Service Task in the compiled worker binding. The annotated parameter name becomes a local variable extracted from the job's variable payload and injected as a typed Java parameter into the handler method. This provides automatic deserialization of process variables into strongly typed Java objects without manual variable extraction from the job variable map.

The @Variable annotation applies to individual handler method parameters in a Spring Zeebe worker, not to global process-wide inputs. It extracts the named variable from the job variables payload for the specific task type the handler processes, scoping the extraction to that handler invocation. It does not affect the overall process variable scope or create process-wide input mappings. The annotation is local to the worker method parameter binding context.

The @Variable annotation does not map the parameter to a BPMN model attribute -- BPMN attributes are static XML properties defined in the process model at design time. @Variable operates in the runtime domain, extracting a process variable from the activated job payload and injecting it as a parameter into the Java handler method at invocation time. No BPMN XML is modified by the annotation -- it is a purely runtime binding mechanism in the Spring Zeebe SDK.

@Variable annotation has a direct runtime effect: it extracts the named variable from the job variable payload and provides it as a typed parameter to the worker handler method, handling JSON deserialization automatically. getVariablesAsMap() or getVariable(name) methods and parse them. @Variable is a convenience annotation that reduces boilerplate variable extraction code, not a cosmetic marker. ###

a.text: Correct. Element Template binding types include zeebe:input (input mapping), zeebe:output (output mapping), zeebe:taskDefinition, zeebe:taskHeader, property, etc. zeebe:input creates an Input Mapping at the BPMN level -- the property's value becomes a local variable. Example from web-modeler/element-templates/best-practices.md:167: "binding": { "type": "zeebe:input", "name": "priority0" }.

b.text: Incorrect. Process-wide is different from task-local Input Mapping.

c.text: Incorrect. Maps to extension element, not core BPMN attribute.

explanation: Element Template binding types include zeebe:input (input mapping), zeebe:output (output mapping), zeebe:taskDefinition, zeebe:taskHeader, property, etc. zeebe:input creates an Input Mapping at the BPMN level -- the property's value becomes a local variable. Example from web-modeler/element-templates/best-practices.md:167: "binding": { "type": "zeebe:input", "name": "priority0" }.
